key_points: Off-campus buildings like The Vantage and The View have serious management failures
Package theft is rampant; USPS delivers to wrong locations constantly
One working elevator for hundreds of residents reported at The View
key_quotes: Never live at The View... one working elevator available for the hundreds of residents for the past few weeks. Half the laundry machines are broken.
That whole building and management company are just a joke. You're better off renting a house off campus.
You truly cannot have shit delivered here, even if you have a mailroom

key_points: Street preachers and hate-speech groups are a recurring campus presence
Basketball games have low attendance; school spirit is inconsistent
Community feel exists but is dampened by safety concerns and stress
key_quotes: I overheard the guy in the red jesus hat telling students that if they're gay they should kill themselves.
Are more than 4 people going to show up?
My work has me over at Villanova often, and it's crazy how much better the energy is at Temple. Always feels good to come back.

key_points: Food trucks are beloved — honey truck and halal options are student favorites
Twisted Taco has a loyal following despite some digestive jokes
Bagels and Co reportedly closed; on-campus dining options shrinking
key_quotes: I love twisted taco. I love u. have a great day
not twisted taco 😭 do you also like liquid shit coming out your butt
My favorite trucks from 18/19... one specific halal truck outside the student center, Top Bap started my love for Korean food!

key_points: Depression and burnout are openly discussed; many students struggling visibly
Tuttleman Counseling is available and has helped students with accommodations
Cherry Pantry food bank exists for students in need — no questions asked
key_quotes: I've been struggling with severe depression since 2017. I've been doing horrible in uni for years because of it.
Please see Tuttleman Counseling if things get too bad. I know they've personally helped me.
multiple students have gotten robbed at gunpoint, one guy got shot in the Chipotle... We've got this.

key_points: Student Financial Services (SFS) is notoriously slow and unresponsive
Pennsylvania state grants can be delayed due to state budget failures
Temple Promise program covers tuition for qualifying low-income students
key_quotes: The SFS problem is actually making me want to transfer... I have to miss classes in order to work and it's making me behind.
If I have to wait months for a refund that should've been processed first week of school.
FYI — Student Financial Services is under the Enrollment Management umbrella, so this is also why your financial aid is a mess.

key_points: Campus is overcrowded with too few study spaces for the student body
Double standard flagged: students expelled for AI use while admin uses it freely
Library is noisy; students report instruments being played inside
key_quotes: There's not enough space on this campus... I often find myself spending half of my hour between classes just wandering looking for a table.
You use AI = fail the class or even expulsion. They use AI = tolerated or even encouraged.
Why are they playing instruments in the library 😭... I'm paying thousands of dollars for a library and not a social space.

key_points: Admissions department lost dozens of staff in two years per published investigation
University used AI-generated art in official materials while punishing students for same
Temple is on a federal watchlist related to campus civil rights investigation
key_quotes: Very telling, good work. Explains why admissions has been a complete disaster.
Leadership and accountability should start at the top.
Honestly a lot of the essential organs of the university have been disasters for a while now.

key_points: North Philly location means real safety concerns — shootings near campus reported
SEPTA cuts are devastating for commuter students who rely on regional rail
Campus is being actively renovated; Beury Hall demolished for green space
key_quotes: I'm gonna catch some flak but I don't think it was a good move to try to turn north filthy into temple town. It really isn't a safe area overall.
Some of us can't afford to dorm. Can't afford to live off campus. I commute from home... Five train lines will be eliminated.
RIP Beury Beach

key_points: Temple is well-recognized in Philadelphia and the Northeast but limited nationally
TUJ alumni report Japanese companies recognizing the degree for local hiring
Temple Promise and scholarship options make ROI strong for in-state students
key_quotes: Most people in the Northeast and Mid-Atlantic are very familiar with Temple but are people from the Midwest, South, and West Coast familiar with Temple?
All the job interviews I have had for large Japanese companies know TUJ. So if you're looking to work in Japan after graduation, this school will do you well.
Temple is still in the Philadelphia area which is a great city for networking.

key_points: Full-tuition Temple Promise program is a genuine game-changer for qualifying students
At $36k/year without aid, multiple Reddit users say it's not worth it
Consistently recommended over taking on $100k+ debt at Penn or similar schools
key_quotes: As another adult who's taken out loans: go to Temple. I would never recommend taking out 100k in loans to anyone.
Is Temple worth 36k/year for its business school?
It is one of the best programs that Temple has ever created IMO. Congratulations! [re: Temple Promise]

key_points: Strong community warmth coexists with serious safety and administrative frustrations
Students rally around shared struggles — mental health, SEPTA, campus crime
Proud Philly identity; students who embrace the city tend to thrive here
key_quotes: I love temple. I love my roommates. I love my friends. I love the library.
This start feels rough. Multiple students have gotten robbed at gunpoint, one guy got shot in the Chipotle, and, you know, [gestures to the world in general]. We've got this.
I graduated in 08 and I loved it all.

key_points: **Safety**: shootings at Chipotle near campus; student beaten in dorm on video
**Administration**: admissions department gutted, financial aid office unresponsive for months
**Housing**: off-campus buildings advertised before construction finished; management companies took deposits and went ghost
key_quotes: Violent teen mob beats Temple University student in dorm in vicious caught-on-video attack
This place is NOT done with construction... they stole money from me and many others and have changed management so many times.
holy shit this is my roommate, keep in mind he asked security for help and they did nothing

key_points: Free Philadelphia art museum membership comes with your student ID
Cherry Pantry in the student center provides free food — no eligibility questions asked
Temple Promise covers full tuition for qualifying Philly students — check eligibility early
key_quotes: Free art museum membership
Reminder that the Cherry Pantry in the student center is available to anyone who needs it! They won't ask questions about why you're using it.
Reach out to people in or adjacent to your field... I was told to do this so often by my advisor, and I didn't get the nerve to do it until grad school.
